notes: Layer register, applied. Stratigraphy / age / depth questions. The technical board, but in question form. THE_QUARRY is teaching here even though he doesn't say so.

threads.subject: what is the age of the binding code?

op.body:
What is the age of the binding code?

The sticky in the barn on CALICHE is dated 1981. Is 1981 the age of the binding code, or is 1981 the age of the sticky?

If the sticky is the third copy, what is the age of the first copy? If the first copy was a copy, what is the age of the original? If the original was not a copy and not original, what was it?

In the layer register, what we are asking is: at what depth was the binding code laid down, and what has been pressed onto it since?

replies.body: Foal — is the sticky the binding code or is the sticky the way to remember the binding code? If the sticky is the way to remember, the age of the sticky is the age of the remembering. The age of the binding code is older than the remembering.

threads.subject: what depth is the cathedral basement?

op.body:
What depth is the cathedral basement at Graustark? Is the depth the depth below grade or the depth below the river? In the layer register the cathedral basement is below the floor of the river, which means the basement is in a stratum the river does not reach. Is that the stratum the wedge is in?

The question is not whether the wedge is there. The question is whether the stratum the wedge is in is the stratum the wedge was always in.

replies.body: Is the question being asked in the way it is being asked because the question is meant to be answered by JF or by me? — F.
Is it being answered by either?

threads.subject: what is the age of the parish's silence?

op.body:
The second-hour list at the parish this year is twenty-two names. Twenty-two names of the parish's dead whose names were not read for reasons the parish now understands were wrong.

What is the age of a name unread?

If the earliest name on the list is 1946, is the silence forty-nine years old, or is the silence as old as the reason?

In the layer register, we count from the top down. The silence is at depth. The reading, this year, is a core-sample.

replies.body: Is the core-sample the reading, or is the core-sample the reader who reads it? — F.
Is the reader who reads at 8:48 PM the same reader who signed up in September?

op.body:
The seed-dying rate in Small Wood this season is six per two months. In the layer register, is that one rate or is that two rates?

If the rate is uniform the register is one stratum. If the rate is punctuated the register is two.

The HOA's schedule (see TG_002) says the rate is uniform. The Lacombe sisters' rate of prayer on the north road is not uniform. The rate of prayer is the observation.

What is the layer register of the sisters' prayer?

replies.body: THE_QUARRY · the sisters walked the same decade on both legs for the first time since '93 this Tuesday. The register you're asking about is the register of when they change decades. That is not what the HOA thinks it's counting. — RH.

threads.subject: answering the matriarch · what age is the remembering

op.body:
WM asked on BL_001: is the age of the sticky the age of the remembering.

In the layer register the remembering is a stratum above the code. The stratum is compacted by every reader who has passed over it. The stratum's density is the density of the compaction. The density is not the age. The density is what has been laid down since.

When the matriarch's sticky is replaced next spring the new sticky will be laid on top of the compacted stratum, not on top of the code. This is what a copy is.

replies.body: Foal — the density is what has been laid down since. Yes. The sticky's job is to hold the stratum steady while a new reader compacts it. That is the sticky's whole labor.
